- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

25	    """Raised when configs/platform.yaml is malformed or missing required keys."""

28	# ── ADR-0002 data-sovereignty: the canonical loopback guard ───────────────────
29	# SCF-derived content (embeddings, traces, experiment metadata) must NEVER leave
30	# the compute node. This is the single shared validator used by EVERY infra module
31	# that takes a host/URI from config — Phoenix, Qdrant, MLflow — so the boundary is
32	# enforced identically everywhere instead of one guard per module (pass-5).
33	LOOPBACK_HOSTS = {"localhost", "127.0.0.1", "::1"}
34	
35	
36	def require_loopback(host: str, what: str) -> str:
37	    """Return host if it is loopback, else raise ConfigError. 0.0.0.0 is rejected:
38	    it binds all interfaces, exposing data off-box — exactly what ADR-0002 forbids."""
39	    if host not in LOOPBACK_HOSTS:
40	        raise ConfigError(
41	            f"{what} host {host!r} is not loopback. SCF-derived content must stay on "
42	            f"the compute node (ADR-0002). Use one of {sorted(LOOPBACK_HOSTS)}."
43	        )

48	    """Validate a backing-store URI is local (ADR-0002). A bare path or sqlite:///
49	    file is local; any networked scheme (http/https/postgresql/mysql/mssql) must
50	    point at a loopback host. Rejects remote tracking servers that would exfiltrate."""
51	    if uri.startswith("sqlite:") or "://" not in uri:
52	        return uri  # local sqlite file or a bare relative directory store
53	    host = (urlparse(uri).hostname or "").strip()

88	        # as a TypeError. Reject it here (pass-7).
89	        if node is None:
90	            raise ConfigError(f"{source}: required config key '{path}' is null.")
91	
92	    # checkpointing.backend selects the checkpointer; an unrecognized value silently
93	    # falls through to the Postgres path. Validate the enum at load (pass-7).
94	    backend = cfg["checkpointing"]["backend"]
95	    if backend not in ("memory", "postgres"):
96	        raise ConfigError(
97	            f"{source}: checkpointing.backend must be 'memory' or 'postgres', got {backend!r}."
98	        )
99	
100	    # ADR-0002 fail-fast: enforce loopback/local on every host/URI value at LOAD
101	    # time, not just at point-of-use, so a misconfigured platform.yaml is rejected
102	    # at startup rather than silently exfiltrating to a remote host (pass-5). These
103	    # are value checks; the loop above only checked key presence.
104	    require_loopback(str(cfg["vector_store"]["host"]), f"{source}: vector_store")
105	    require_local_uri(
106	        str(cfg["observability"]["mlflow"]["tracking_uri"]), f"{source}: observability.mlflow"
107	    )
108	    phoenix = cfg["observability"].get("phoenix", {})
109	    if isinstance(phoenix, dict) and "host" in phoenix:
110	        require_loopback(str(phoenix["host"]), f"{source}: observability.phoenix")
111	    checkpointing = cfg.get("checkpointing", {})
112	    if isinstance(checkpointing, dict) and "host" in checkpointing:
113	        require_loopback(str(checkpointing["host"]), f"{source}: checkpointing")
114	    return cfg
115	
116	
117	@functools.lru_cache(maxsize=1)
118	def load_platform_config() -> dict[str, Any]:
119	    """
120	    Load, validate, and cache configs/platform.yaml.
121	
122	    Cached for the process lifetime — platform-scope config is stable within a
123	    run. Call load_platform_config.cache_clear() in tests that mutate the file.
124	    Raises ConfigError if required sections are missing.
125	    """
126	    config_path = repo_root() / "configs" / "platform.yaml"
127	    if not config_path.exists():
128	        raise FileNotFoundError(
129	            f"Platform config not found at {config_path}. "
130	            "Infrastructure modules require configs/platform.yaml."
131	        )
132	    with config_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
133	        cfg = yaml.safe_load(fh)
134	    return _validate_config(cfg, str(config_path))

1	"""
2	reasoning_client — the ADR-0016 LLM execution-routing seam.
3	
4	A single abstraction that isolates LLM auth/transport from agent logic. Agents
5	declare *what* to reason about and at *what stakes tier*; this seam decides
6	*where* the call runs, how it authenticates, how it retries, times out, and how
7	it is traced. No agent imports an LLM client directly — that coupling lives here
8	and only here (ADR-0016 §"Implementation seam").
9	
10	Tiers (routing is by stakes, per ADR-0016 / agents/MODEL_TIER_ASSIGNMENTS.md):
11	
12	    Tier.T1  — Deterministic ML. No LLM. complete() on T1 is a routing bug and
13	               raises: T1 work is computation, never inference.
14	    Tier.T2  — Local LLM on the Mac mini, 24/7 (Ollama). Bulk / low-stakes
15	               reasoning: per-pair classification, triage, drafting, review.
16	    Tier.T3  — Frontier Claude Max 20x via claude-agent-sdk. The `claude` CLI's
17	               subscription OAuth (CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN) is the ONLY auth path
18	               — NO Anthropic API key. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is scrubbed from the SDK
19	               subprocess env so a stray key can never spill to the metered API
20	               (ADR-0016). Reserved for high-stakes judgments. Bursty / queued.
21	
22	Reliability (hardening pass 1):
23	  - Every call has a per-tier timeout (#145, #158) so a hung backend can never
24	    block a synchronous LangGraph node forever.
25	  - Transient failures are retried with bounded exponential backoff (#157).
26	  - Every call is wrapped in an OpenTelemetry span carrying run_id/agent_id/tier
27	    (OpenInference LLM conventions) so BOTH tiers are visible in Phoenix — T3 is
28	    not LangChain-instrumented, so without this it would be invisible (#150, #168).
29	    Spans are cheap no-ops when no tracer provider is registered (offline/tests).
30	  - complete_json uses Ollama's native JSON mode on T2 and a balanced-brace
31	    extractor, instead of a greedy regex that mangled multi-value replies (#159).
32	
33	Data sovereignty (ADR-0002 as amended by ADR-0016): persistent artifacts never
34	leave the machine; T3 permits transient, non-retained inference over the specific
35	SCF-derived text needed for a single call.
36	
37	Public surface:
38	    complete(tier, prompt, system=..., run_id=..., agent_id=...) -> str
39	    complete_json(tier, prompt, ...) -> dict | list
40	    is_available(tier) -> bool
41	"""
42	
43	from __future__ import annotations
44	
45	import asyncio
46	import json
47	import os
48	import threading
49	import time
50	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
51	from concurrent.futures import TimeoutError as FuturesTimeout
52	from enum import Enum
53	from functools import lru_cache
54	from typing import Any
55	
56	from opentelemetry import trace
57	
58	# ── Tiers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
59	
60	
61	class Tier(str, Enum):
62	    """Execution tier, by stakes. See agents/MODEL_TIER_ASSIGNMENTS.md."""
63	
64	    T1 = "t1"  # deterministic ML — no LLM
65	    T2 = "t2"  # local Ollama
66	    T3 = "t3"  # frontier Claude Max via Agent SDK
67	
68	
69	class ReasoningError(RuntimeError):
70	    """Raised when a reasoning call cannot be completed (backend down, timeout, T1 misuse)."""
71	
72	
73	class PermanentReasoningError(ReasoningError):
74	    """A non-transient failure (model missing, auth, CLI absent). Never retried."""
75	
76	
77	class RateLimitError(ReasoningError):
78	    """A Tier-3 subscription rate window. Retried with long backpressure, never
79	    spilled to a metered API (ADR-0016)."""
80	
81	
82	# ── Configuration (env-overridable; local-first defaults) ─────────────────────
83	
84	
85	def _env(name: str, default: str) -> str:
86	    val = os.environ.get(name, "").strip()
87	    return val or default
88	
89	
90	T2_MODEL = _env("HCGRC_T2_MODEL", "llama3.1:8b")
91	T2_BASE_URL = _env("HCGRC_OLLAMA_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:11434")
92	T3_MODEL = os.environ.get("HCGRC_T3_MODEL", "").strip() or None
93	
94	# Per-tier hard timeouts (seconds). T3 is bursty/queued so it gets more headroom.
95	T2_TIMEOUT = float(_env("HCGRC_T2_TIMEOUT", "120"))
96	T3_TIMEOUT = float(_env("HCGRC_T3_TIMEOUT", "300"))
97	
98	# Tier-2 (Ollama) decoding controls. num_ctx avoids silent truncation at the 2048
99	# default (#196); seed makes greedy decoding reproducible (#197); num_predict caps
100	# output so a runaway/JSON-mode generation hits a bound before the hard timeout (#198).
101	T2_NUM_CTX = int(_env("HCGRC_T2_NUM_CTX", "8192"))
102	T2_SEED = int(_env("HCGRC_T2_SEED", "0"))
103	T2_NUM_PREDICT = int(_env("HCGRC_T2_NUM_PREDICT", "2048"))
104	
105	# Bounded retry with exponential backoff on transient failures. Clamped >= 0 so a
106	# negative override cannot produce an empty loop that raises None (#208).
107	MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("HCGRC_REASONING_RETRIES", "2")))
108	BACKOFF_BASE = float(_env("HCGRC_REASONING_BACKOFF", "1.5"))
109	# Tier-3 rate-window backpressure: long, capped waits — queue and resume, never
110	# fail fast onto a metered path.
111	RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF = float(_env("HCGRC_RATELIMIT_BACKOFF", "30"))
112	RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("HCGRC_RATELIMIT_RETRIES", "5")))
113	
114	# Truncation cap for prompt/response text recorded on spans (local Phoenix only).
115	_SPAN_TEXT_CAP = 8000
116	
117	_tracer = trace.get_tracer("hcgrc.reasoning_client")
118	
119	# Shared executor for enforcing sync-call timeouts. Not shut down: a hung backend
120	# call leaks one daemon worker rather than blocking the node (a ThreadPoolExecutor
121	# context-manager exit would join the hung thread, defeating the timeout).
122	_TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS = 8
123	_timeout_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(
124	    max_workers=_TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout"
125	)
126	
127	# In-flight worker accounting (#272). A timed-out call abandons (does NOT cancel)
128	# its worker — ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread blocked in
129	# llm.invoke/asyncio.run — so a truly-hung backend leaks that slot until (if ever)
130	# the call returns. Without a bound, accumulated leaks saturate the 8-slot pool and
131	# every subsequent submit silently queues behind work that never starts, converting
132	# ALL traffic into guaranteed timeouts. We track in-flight slots and fail FAST and
133	# LOUD with a distinct error when the pool is saturated, instead of degrading into a
134	# silent cliff. The per-tier inner timeouts (T2 HTTP timeout, T3 asyncio.wait_for)
135	# normally let workers self-terminate, so this guard only fires under genuine leaks.
136	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
137	_inflight = 0
138	
139	
140	def _submit_bounded(fn):
141	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if all worker slots are already in
142	    flight (i.e. leaked to hung backends). Decrements the count when the future
143	    completes — a leaked worker keeps its slot counted until it finally returns."""
144	    global _inflight
145	    with _inflight_lock:
146	        if _inflight >= _TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS:
147	            # Permanent, not transient: every worker is leaked to a hung backend, so
148	            # retrying with short backoff just wastes the retry budget before failing
149	            # anyway. Fail fast and loud (pass-7) — PermanentReasoningError breaks
150	            # complete()'s retry loop immediately.
151	            raise PermanentReasoningError(
152	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS} "
153	                "workers in-flight) — hung backend call(s) have leaked every worker slot"
154	            )
155	        _inflight += 1
156	
157	    def _release(_):
158	        global _inflight
159	        with _inflight_lock:
160	            _inflight -= 1
161	
162	    # Submit only after incrementing, but if submit() itself raises (e.g. the pool
163	    # was shut down — "cannot schedule new futures after shutdown") the done-callback
164	    # never fires, so decrement here to avoid permanently leaking the slot and
165	    # eventually false-saturating the pool (pass-5).
166	    try:
167	        future = _timeout_pool.submit(fn)
168	    except BaseException:
169	        with _inflight_lock:
170	            _inflight -= 1
171	        raise
172	    future.add_done_callback(_release)
173	    return future
174	
175	
176	# ── Timeout helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
177	
178	
179	def _with_timeout(fn, timeout: float, what: str):
180	    """Run a sync callable with a hard timeout; raise ReasoningError on overrun.
181	
182	    Propagates the current OpenTelemetry context into the worker thread so spans
183	    created by fn (e.g. the LangChain ChatOllama span) nest under the active
184	    reasoning.complete span instead of being orphaned (#238).
185	    """
186	    from opentelemetry import context as otel_context
187	
188	    ctx = otel_context.get_current()
189	
190	    def _run():
191	        token = otel_context.attach(ctx)
192	        try:
193	            return fn()
194	        finally:
195	            otel_context.detach(token)
196	
197	    future = _submit_bounded(_run)
198	    try:
199	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
200	    except FuturesTimeout as exc:
201	        raise ReasoningError(f"{what} timed out after {timeout:.0f}s") from exc
202	
203	
204	def _run_sync(coro: Any) -> Any:
205	    """Run a coroutine to completion from sync code, even inside a running loop."""
206	    try:
207	        asyncio.get_running_loop()
208	    except RuntimeError:
209	        return asyncio.run(coro)
210	    # Bound the cross-thread wait so a starved/leaked pool can't deadlock a node
211	    # forever (#227). The inner coroutine already has its own asyncio timeout; this
212	    # is a backstop slightly beyond it. _submit_bounded fails fast if the pool is
213	    # already saturated with leaked workers rather than queueing forever (#272).
214	    return _submit_bounded(lambda: asyncio.run(coro)).result(timeout=T3_TIMEOUT + 30)
215	
216	
217	# ── Tier 2: local Ollama ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
218	
219	
220	@lru_cache(maxsize=16)
221	def _ollama(model: str, temperature: float, json_mode: bool) -> Any:
222	    """Construct (and cache) a ChatOllama client for the local serving stack."""
223	    try:
224	        from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
225	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - dependency guard
226	        raise ReasoningError(
227	            "langchain-ollama is not installed — Tier 2 backend unavailable."
228	        ) from exc
229	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {
230	        "model": model,
231	        "temperature": temperature,
232	        "base_url": T2_BASE_URL,
233	        "num_ctx": T2_NUM_CTX,         # avoid silent truncation at the 2048 default (#196)
234	        "seed": T2_SEED,               # reproducible greedy decoding (#197)
235	        "client_kwargs": {"timeout": T2_TIMEOUT},  # native HTTP timeout (#195)
236	    }
237	    if json_mode:
238	        # Native structured-output: Ollama constrains decoding to valid JSON,
239	        # far more reliable than instructing + regex-extracting (#159, #175). Do
240	        # NOT cap num_predict here — format=json terminates at the end of a valid
241	        # JSON value, and a num_predict cap would truncate it into INVALID JSON
242	        # (#231). The hard timeout remains the runaway backstop.
243	        kwargs["format"] = "json"
244	    else:
245	        kwargs["num_predict"] = T2_NUM_PREDICT  # bound free-text output length (#198)
246	    return ChatOllama(**kwargs)
247	
248	
249	def _t2_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, temperature: float, model: str,
250	                 json_mode: bool) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
251	    # ADR-0002: the prompt is SCF-derived — refuse to send it to a non-loopback
252	    # Ollama endpoint (HCGRC_OLLAMA_BASE_URL could point off-box) (pass-7).
253	    from .infrastructure.config import require_local_uri
254	    require_local_uri(T2_BASE_URL, "Tier-2 Ollama (HCGRC_OLLAMA_BASE_URL)")
255	    llm = _ollama(model, temperature, json_mode)
256	    messages: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
257	    if system:
258	        messages.append(("system", system))
259	    messages.append(("human", prompt))
260	
261	    def _call():
262	        resp = llm.invoke(messages)
263	        content = getattr(resp, "content", resp)
264	        text = content if isinstance(content, str) else str(content)
265	        return text, getattr(resp, "usage_metadata", None)  # token counts (#279)
266	
267	    try:
268	        # Give the outer future timeout margin over the inner HTTP timeout
269	        # (client_kwargs timeout == T2_TIMEOUT) so the inner socket timeout wins and
270	        # the worker self-terminates cleanly, instead of racing exactly and risking
271	        # an abandoned (leaked) worker — mirrors the T3_TIMEOUT + 30 backstop (#272).
272	        text, usage = _with_timeout(
273	            _call, T2_TIMEOUT + 10, f"Tier 2 (Ollama @ {T2_BASE_URL})"
274	        )
275	        text = text.strip()
276	        if not text:
277	            # An empty/whitespace reply is not a valid result — surface it as a
278	            # retryable error rather than silently returning "" and corrupting a
279	            # JSON caller or recording an empty high-stakes answer (#233).
280	            raise ReasoningError(f"Tier 2 (Ollama @ {T2_BASE_URL}) returned an empty reply")
281	        return text, {"model": model, "usage": usage}
282	    except ReasoningError:
283	        raise
284	    except Exception as exc:
285	        msg = str(exc).lower()
286	        # A missing model is permanent — retrying just wastes backoff (#194).
287	        if "not found" in msg or "no such model" in msg or "try pulling" in msg:
288	            raise PermanentReasoningError(
289	                f"Tier 2 model {model!r} not available on Ollama: {exc}"
290	            ) from exc
291	        raise ReasoningError(f"Tier 2 (Ollama @ {T2_BASE_URL}) call failed: {exc}") from exc
292	
293	
294	# ── Tier 3: frontier Claude Max via Agent SDK ─────────────────────────────────
295	
296	
297	def _scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
298	    """
299	    Environment for the Agent SDK subprocess with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY removed.
300	
301	    ADR-0016: execution runs entirely on the fixed Claude Max subscription via the
302	    `claude` CLI's OAuth token. A metered Anthropic API key must NEVER be used — if
303	    one is present in the process env it would silently bill per-token, so we strip
304	    it before the SDK ever sees it.
305	    """
306	    return {k: v for k, v in os.environ.items() if k != "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"}
307	
308	
309	async def _t3_async(prompt: str, system: str | None, max_turns: int,
310	                    model: str | None) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
311	    try:
312	        from claude_agent_sdk import (
313	            AssistantMessage,
314	            ClaudeAgentOptions,
315	            ResultMessage,
316	            TextBlock,
317	            query,
318	        )
319	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - dependency guard
320	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
321	            "claude-agent-sdk is not installed — Tier 3 backend unavailable."
322	        ) from exc
323	
324	    opts = ClaudeAgentOptions(
325	        max_turns=max_turns,
326	        system_prompt=system,
327	        allowed_tools=[],          # pure reasoning — no filesystem/command access
328	        env=_scrubbed_env(),       # ADR-0016: no metered API key (#146)
329	        setting_sources=[],        # don't load ANY on-disk settings into the
330	                                   # subprocess — keep the sandbox closed (#12)
331	        permission_mode="dontAsk",  # never wait on an interactive permission prompt
332	                                    # in this headless, single-turn, no-tools path —
333	                                    # deny if not pre-approved so any attempted tool
334	                                    # use fails fast instead of hanging until the
335	                                    # T3_TIMEOUT backstop (#274)
336	    )
337	    if model:
338	        opts.model = model
339	
340	    chunks: list[str] = []
341	    meta: dict[str, Any] = {"model": model}  # real model id if pinned, else unknown
342	    result_error: dict[str, Any] | None = None
343	
344	    async def _drain():
345	        nonlocal result_error
346	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=opts):
347	            # The SDK emits a first-class RateLimitEvent ahead of / instead of a
348	            # ResultMessage error. ONLY rate_limit_info.status == 'rejected' is a hard
349	            # rate window — 'allowed'/'allowed_warning' are informational and must NOT
350	            # trigger the 30s+ backpressure path (pass-6, SDK contract). Keep a generic
351	            # status=='rejected' fallback for other rejected-message shapes (#266).
352	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
353	                rl_status = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
354	                if rl_status == "rejected":
355	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "errors": ["RateLimitEvent"],
356	                                    "stop_reason": "rate_limit"}
357	                continue
358	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
359	                result_error = {"status": 429, "errors": ["rejected"], "stop_reason": "rate_limit"}
360	                continue
361	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
362	                if getattr(msg, "model", None):
363	                    meta["model"] = msg.model  # the actual model the run used (#204)
364	                for block in msg.content:
365	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
366	                        chunks.append(block.text)
367	            elif isinstance(msg, ResultMessage):
368	                if getattr(msg, "usage", None):
369	                    meta["usage"] = msg.usage  # token usage for the span (#203)
370	                if getattr(msg, "total_cost_usd", None) is not None:
371	                    meta["cost_usd"] = msg.total_cost_usd
372	                # Capture the model's stop reason on SUCCESSFUL runs too (not just
373	                # errors) so the span records why generation ended — end_turn vs
374	                # max_tokens vs tool_use (OpenInference llm.finish_reason) (pass-5).
375	                if getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None):
376	                    meta["stop_reason"] = msg.stop_reason
377	                # The CLI reports API failures (incl. rate windows) by COMPLETING
378	                # the stream with is_error=True / api_error_status set, NOT by
379	                # raising. Capture it so a failed run is not returned as success
380	                # (pass-4 #1). But do NOT clobber a rate-limit (429) already captured
381	                # from a RateLimitEvent — a trailing ResultMessage with a different
382	                # api_error_status would otherwise downgrade the signal and lose the
383	                # long-backpressure path (pass-7).
384	                if getattr(msg, "is_error", False) and not (
385	                    result_error and result_error.get("status") == 429
386	                ):
387	                    result_error = {
388	                        "status": getattr(msg, "api_error_status", None),
389	                        "errors": getattr(msg, "errors", None),
390	                        "stop_reason": getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
391	                    }
392	
393	    await asyncio.wait_for(_drain(), timeout=T3_TIMEOUT)
394	
395	    if result_error is not None:
396	        status = result_error.get("status")
397	        detail = result_error.get("errors") or result_error.get("stop_reason") or status
398	        if status in (429, 529):
399	            raise RateLimitError(f"Tier 3 rate window (api_error_status={status}): {detail}")
400	        if status in (401, 403):
401	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"Tier 3 auth failure ({status}): {detail}")
402	        raise ReasoningError(f"Tier 3 run reported an error (status={status}): {detail}")
403	
404	    return "".join(chunks).strip(), meta
405	
406	
407	def _t3_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, max_turns: int,
408	                 model: str | None) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
409	    try:
410	        text, meta = _run_sync(_t3_async(prompt, system, max_turns, model))
411	        if not text:
412	            # An empty frontier reply is not a valid high-stakes result — retry
413	            # rather than return "" as success (#246).
414	            raise ReasoningError("Tier 3 (Agent SDK) returned an empty reply")
415	        return text, meta
416	    except ReasoningError:
417	        raise
418	    except (asyncio.TimeoutError, FuturesTimeout) as exc:
419	        raise ReasoningError(f"Tier 3 (Agent SDK) timed out after {T3_TIMEOUT:.0f}s") from exc
420	    except Exception as exc:
421	        # Classify on both the message and the exception type name — substring
422	        # matching alone is fragile across SDK versions (#229).
423	        sig = f"{type(exc).__name__} {exc}".lower()
424	        # Subscription rate window — queue and resume with long backpressure,
425	        # never spill to a metered API (ADR-0016, #185).
426	        if any(k in sig for k in ("rate limit", "rate_limit", "ratelimit", "429",
427	                                  "overloaded", "usage limit", "too many requests")):
428	            raise RateLimitError(f"Tier 3 rate window: {exc}") from exc
429	        # CLI absent / auth failure — permanent, not worth retrying (#194).
430	        if any(k in sig for k in ("not found", "notfound", "command not found",
431	                                  "no such file", "auth", "credential", "401",
432	                                  "403", "unauthor")):
433	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"Tier 3 (Agent SDK) unavailable: {exc}") from exc
434	        raise ReasoningError(f"Tier 3 (Agent SDK) call failed: {exc}") from exc
435	
436	
437	# ── Public API ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
438	
439	
440	def _attempt(tier: Tier, prompt: str, system: str | None, temperature: float,
441	             max_turns: int, model: str | None, json_mode: bool) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
442	    if tier is Tier.T2:
443	        return _t2_complete(prompt, system, temperature, model or T2_MODEL, json_mode)
444	    return _t3_complete(prompt, system, max_turns, model or T3_MODEL)
445	
446	
447	def _record_usage(span, usage: Any) -> None:
448	    """Set OpenInference token-count attributes from a usage dict/object (#203)."""
449	    if not usage:
450	        return
451	
452	    def g(key: str):
453	        return usage.get(key) if isinstance(usage, dict) else getattr(usage, key, None)
454	
455	    prompt_toks = g("input_tokens")
456	    completion_toks = g("output_tokens")
457	    # Anthropic/Agent-SDK report cache tokens in SEPARATE fields excluded from
458	    # input_tokens; on a cached-prefix run they can dominate real input. Fold them
459	    # into the prompt/total counts (and emit the OpenInference cache slots) so
460	    # llm.token_count.total reflects all tokens processed, not an undercount on the
461	    # very tier where usage visibility matters most (#280). Real spend is still
462	    # tracked separately via llm.cost_usd.
463	    cache_read = g("cache_read_input_tokens")
464	    cache_write = g("cache_creation_input_tokens")
465	    if cache_read is not None:
466	        span.set_attribute("llm.token_count.prompt_details.cache_read", cache_read)
467	    if cache_write is not None:
468	        span.set_attribute("llm.token_count.prompt_details.cache_write", cache_write)
469	
470	    prompt_total = None
471	    if prompt_toks is not None or cache_read is not None or cache_write is not None:
472	        prompt_total = (prompt_toks or 0) + (cache_read or 0) + (cache_write or 0)
473	        span.set_attribute("llm.token_count.prompt", prompt_total)
474	    if completion_toks is not None:
475	        span.set_attribute("llm.token_count.completion", completion_toks)
476	    if prompt_total is not None and completion_toks is not None:
477	        span.set_attribute("llm.token_count.total", prompt_total + completion_toks)
478	
479	
480	def complete(
481	    tier: Tier,
482	    prompt: str,
483	    *,
484	    system: str | None = None,
485	    temperature: float = 0.0,
486	    max_turns: int = 1,
487	    model: str | None = None,
488	    run_id: str | None = None,
489	    agent_id: str | None = None,
490	    _json_mode: bool = False,
491	) -> str:
492	    """
493	    Run a single reasoning call at the given stakes tier and return the text.
494	
495	    Times out per tier, retries transient failures with backoff, and emits an
496	    OpenTelemetry span (run_id/agent_id/tier) so the call is visible in Phoenix.
497	
498	    Raises ReasoningError on T1 misuse or after retries are exhausted. Callers
499	    that must keep the graph moving (dry-runs) should catch and degrade.
500	    """
501	    if tier is Tier.T1:
502	        raise ReasoningError(
503	            "Tier.T1 is deterministic ML — it has no LLM. A reasoning_client call "
504	            "on T1 is a routing bug; the work belongs in computation, not inference."
505	        )
506	    if os.environ.get("HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING", "").strip():
507	        raise ReasoningError("reasoning disabled via HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING")
508	
509	    # Fall back to the run_id stamped in OTel baggage by run_trace_context, so a
510	    # seam span carries the run_id even when the caller didn't pass it (#239).
511	    if run_id is None:
512	        try:
513	            from opentelemetry import baggage
514	            run_id = baggage.get_baggage("hcgrc.run_id") or None
515	        except Exception:
516	            run_id = None
517	
518	    # Known model id only — never fabricate one (#204). For a T3 run on the
519	    # subscription default, the real model is read back from the response.
520	    known_model = model or (T2_MODEL if tier is Tier.T2 else T3_MODEL)
521	    with _tracer.start_as_current_span("reasoning.complete") as span:
522	        # For T2, LangChainInstrumentor emits a child ChatOllama span under this
523	        # one — intentional parent/child nesting (the seam-level call + the
524	        # provider call), not redundant double-tracing (#214). T3 has no LangChain
525	        # span, so this is the only record of the frontier call.
526	        # OpenInference LLM conventions + HC-GRC cross-store correlation.
527	        span.set_attribute("openinference.span.kind", "LLM")
528	        span.set_attribute("llm.provider", "ollama" if tier is Tier.T2 else "anthropic")
529	        if known_model:
530	            span.set_attribute("llm.model_name", known_model)
531	        span.set_attribute("hcgrc.tier", tier.value)
532	        if run_id:
533	            span.set_attribute("hcgrc.run_id", run_id)
534	            span.set_attribute("session.id", run_id)
535	        if agent_id:
536	            span.set_attribute("hcgrc.agent_id", agent_id)
537	        if system:
538	            # Record the system prompt too — without it the trace input is
539	            # incomplete and the call is not reproducible from the span (#240).
540	            span.set_attribute("llm.system", system[:_SPAN_TEXT_CAP])
541	        span.set_attribute("input.value", prompt[:_SPAN_TEXT_CAP])
542	
543	        last_exc: ReasoningError | None = None
544	        # Independent retry budgets so a mix of transient and rate-limit errors
545	        # cannot exhaust each other's allowance prematurely (#228).
546	        transient_attempts = 0
547	        rate_attempts = 0
548	        while True:
549	            try:
550	                out, meta = _attempt(tier, prompt, system, temperature, max_turns, model, _json_mode)
551	                if meta.get("model"):
552	                    span.set_attribute("llm.model_name", meta["model"])
553	                _record_usage(span, meta.get("usage"))
554	                if meta.get("cost_usd") is not None:
555	                    span.set_attribute("llm.cost_usd", meta["cost_usd"])
556	                if meta.get("stop_reason"):
557	                    span.set_attribute("llm.finish_reason", meta["stop_reason"])  # (pass-5)
558	                span.set_attribute("output.value", out[:_SPAN_TEXT_CAP])
559	                span.set_attribute("llm.retries", transient_attempts + rate_attempts)
560	                return out
561	            except PermanentReasoningError as exc:
562	                # Deterministic failure — do not retry (#194).
563	                last_exc = exc
564	                break
565	            except RateLimitError as exc:
566	                last_exc = exc
567	                if rate_attempts >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES:
568	                    break
569	                sleep_s = RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * (rate_attempts + 1)
570	                span.add_event("rate_limit_backpressure",
571	                               {"attempt": rate_attempts, "sleep_s": sleep_s})  # actual wait (#273)
572	                time.sleep(sleep_s)
573	                rate_attempts += 1
574	            except ReasoningError as exc:
575	                last_exc = exc
576	                if transient_attempts >= MAX_RETRIES:
577	                    break
578	                span.add_event("retry", {"attempt": transient_attempts, "error": str(exc)[:300]})
579	                time.sleep(BACKOFF_BASE ** transient_attempts)
580	                transient_attempts += 1
581	        # Exhausted retries. Record the retry count on the failure span too, for
582	        # parity with the success path (pass-7).
583	        span.set_attribute("llm.retries", transient_attempts + rate_attempts)
584	        span.record_exception(last_exc)
585	        span.set_status(trace.StatusCode.ERROR, str(last_exc))
586	        raise last_exc
587	
588	
589	def complete_json(
590	    tier: Tier,
591	    prompt: str,
592	    *,
593	    system: str | None = None,
594	    temperature: float = 0.0,
595	    max_turns: int = 1,
596	    model: str | None = None,
597	    run_id: str | None = None,
598	    agent_id: str | None = None,
599	) -> Any:
600	    """
601	    Like complete(), but parse the model's reply as JSON.
602	
603	    On T2 this uses Ollama's native JSON mode (constrained decoding). The reply is
604	    then parsed, falling back to a balanced-brace extractor for models that wrap
605	    JSON in prose/fences. A parse failure is retried (re-generating the reply) up
606	    to MAX_RETRIES — the retry budget belongs here, not only in complete() (#230).
607	    Each retry bumps the decoding temperature (#275): with a fixed seed + temp 0,
608	    Ollama decoding is deterministic, so re-issuing the identical prompt would yield
609	    a byte-identical bad reply and the regenerate-on-parse-failure contract could
610	    never recover. A small bump yields a distinct cached client and a genuinely
611	    different generation. (T3 ignores temperature and is non-deterministic anyway.)
612	    Raises ReasoningError if no valid JSON can be recovered.
613	    """
614	    json_system = (system + "\n\n" if system else "") + (
615	        "Respond with ONLY a single valid JSON value and no other text, "
616	        "no markdown fences, no commentary."
617	    )
618	    # Fall back to the run_id stamped in OTel baggage, so the complete_json span
619	    # carries it even when the caller didn't pass it — mirrors complete() (pass-7).
620	    if run_id is None:
621	        try:
622	            from opentelemetry import baggage
623	            run_id = baggage.get_baggage("hcgrc.run_id") or None
624	        except Exception:
625	            run_id = None
626	    last_exc: ReasoningError | None = None
627	    # Span the parse-retry loop so bad-JSON regenerations are visible in Phoenix
628	    # rather than silent (#269).
629	    with _tracer.start_as_current_span("reasoning.complete_json") as span:
630	        span.set_attribute("hcgrc.tier", tier.value)
631	        if run_id:
632	            span.set_attribute("hcgrc.run_id", run_id)
633	            span.set_attribute("session.id", run_id)  # mirror complete() (pass-6)
634	        if agent_id:
635	            # Mirror complete()'s child span so the parent carries the same
636	            # correlation id, not just its children (pass-5).
637	            span.set_attribute("hcgrc.agent_id", agent_id)
638	        for attempt in range(MAX_RETRIES + 1):
639	            # Vary decoding across regenerations so a retry can actually differ from
640	            # the prior attempt (#275). A plain min(1.0, temp + bump) saturates at
641	            # 1.0 for high input temperatures, making retries identical again
642	            # (pass-5); when the upward bump would exceed 1.0, bump DOWNWARD instead
643	            # so every attempt gets a distinct, in-range temperature.
644	            if attempt == 0:
645	                attempt_temp = temperature
646	            else:
647	                bump = 0.2 * attempt
648	                attempt_temp = (temperature + bump if temperature + bump <= 1.0
649	                                else max(0.0, temperature - bump))
650	            try:
651	                raw = complete(
652	                    tier, prompt, system=json_system, temperature=attempt_temp,
653	                    max_turns=max_turns, model=model, run_id=run_id, agent_id=agent_id,
654	                    _json_mode=True,
655	                )
656	            except ReasoningError as exc:
657	                # A backend failure from complete() (rate limit / permanent / other,
658	                # already internally retried) is NOT a JSON parse failure — record it
659	                # on this span too (else only complete()'s child span has it) and
660	                # propagate (pass-5).
661	                span.record_exception(exc)
662	                span.set_status(trace.StatusCode.ERROR, str(exc))
663	                raise
664	            try:
665	                result = _extract_json(raw)
666	                span.set_attribute("json.parse_attempts", attempt + 1)
667	                return result
668	            except ReasoningError as exc:
669	                last_exc = exc  # bad/garbled JSON — regenerate and try again
670	                span.add_event("json_parse_failure",
671	                               {"attempt": attempt, "error": str(exc)[:300]})
672	        span.record_exception(last_exc)
673	        span.set_status(trace.StatusCode.ERROR, str(last_exc))
674	        raise last_exc
675	
676	
677	def _extract_json(text: str) -> Any:
678	    """Recover a JSON value from a model reply that may include fences/prose."""
679	    candidate = text.strip()
680	    # Strip a ```json ... ``` fence if present.
681	    if candidate.startswith("```"):
682	        inner = candidate[3:]
683	        if inner[:4].lower() == "json":
684	            inner = inner[4:]
685	        candidate = inner.rsplit("```", 1)[0].strip()
686	    try:
687	        return json.loads(candidate)
688	    except json.JSONDecodeError:
689	        pass
690	    span = _first_balanced_json(candidate)
691	    if span is not None:
692	        try:
693	            return json.loads(span)
694	        except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
695	            raise ReasoningError(f"Reply was not valid JSON: {text[:200]!r}") from exc
696	    raise ReasoningError(f"No JSON value found in reply: {text[:200]!r}")
697	
698	
699	def _first_balanced_json(text: str) -> str | None:
700	    """
701	    Return the first balanced {...} or [...] span, respecting string literals.
702	
703	    A greedy regex (`[\\{\\[].*[\\}\\]]`) grabs from the first brace to the LAST
704	    brace, which mangles replies containing multiple JSON values or braces in
705	    trailing prose. This walks the structure honestly (#159).
706	    """
707	    start = None
708	    for i, ch in enumerate(text):
709	        if ch in "{[":
710	            start = i
711	            break
712	    if start is None:
713	        return None
714	    open_ch = text[start]
715	    close_ch = "}" if open_ch == "{" else "]"
716	    depth = 0
717	    in_str = False
718	    escaped = False
719	    for i in range(start, len(text)):
720	        ch = text[i]
721	        if in_str:
722	            if escaped:
723	                escaped = False
724	            elif ch == "\\":
725	                escaped = True
726	            elif ch == '"':
727	                in_str = False
728	            continue
729	        if ch == '"':
730	            in_str = True
731	        elif ch == open_ch:
732	            depth += 1
733	        elif ch == close_ch:
734	            depth -= 1
735	            if depth == 0:
736	                return text[start:i + 1]
737	    return None
738	
739	
740	def is_available(tier: Tier) -> bool:
741	    """
742	    Cheap, side-effect-light liveness check for a tier's backend, used by nodes
743	    that must degrade gracefully rather than fail the run.
744	
745	    Does NOT spend an LLM turn (the old probe burned a real Tier-3 frontier turn
746	    on every call, #174, and could hang with no timeout, #173/#30). T1 is always
747	    available (local computation). Honors HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING.
748	    """
749	    if tier is Tier.T1:
750	        return True
751	    if os.environ.get("HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING", "").strip():
752	        return False
753	    if tier is Tier.T2:
754	        # Hit Ollama's /api/tags — no generation, short timeout — and confirm the
755	        # configured model is actually pulled, not merely that the server is up (#210).
756	        import json as _json
757	        import urllib.request
758	        try:
759	            with urllib.request.urlopen(f"{T2_BASE_URL}/api/tags", timeout=3) as r:
760	                if r.status != 200:
761	                    return False
762	                names = [m.get("name", "") for m in _json.loads(r.read()).get("models", [])]
763	        except Exception:
764	            return False
765	        # Exact match only: a prefix match (e.g. configured llama3.1:8b vs a
766	        # pulled llama3.1:70b) would pass here but complete() would then fail as
767	        # "model not found" (#232). Ollama tags an untagged pull as ":latest".
768	        return any(n == T2_MODEL or n == f"{T2_MODEL}:latest" for n in names)
769	    if tier is Tier.T3:
770	        # The `claude` CLI must be present and an OAuth token / stored creds
771	        # available — no frontier turn spent.
772	        import shutil
773	        if shutil.which("claude") is None:
774	            return False
775	        if os.environ.get("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", "").strip():
776	            return True
777	        creds = os.path.expanduser("~/.claude/.credentials.json")
778	        return os.path.exists(creds)
779	    return False

1	"""
2	Phoenix observability bootstrap for HC-GRC (roadmap Phase 0, deliverable #2).
3	
4	ADR-0009: Phoenix is the local, self-hosted LLM-tracing stack (LangSmith was
5	disqualified as SaaS-only). The Phoenix server runs on the compute node and
6	agent LLM calls export OpenTelemetry traces to it.
7	
8	Two responsibilities, deliberately separated:
9	
10	  1. The SERVER is a long-lived process — run it via the `phoenix serve` CLI
11	     (see launch_server_command()) or the launchd plist in scripts/. It is NOT
12	     launched from inside the application process.
13	
14	  2. INSTRUMENTATION is wired per-process via bootstrap_observability(), called
15	     once by each run entrypoint. It registers a global OTel tracer provider
16	     (BatchSpanProcessor — spans are buffered and exported in the background, so
17	     a momentary server outage drops spans rather than blocking or failing the
18	     run) pointed at the running server, and turns on the LangChain instrumentor.
19	     Because the provider is registered globally, the spans the reasoning_client
20	     emits for BOTH tiers — including Tier-3 (claude-agent-sdk), which LangChain
21	     instrumentation does not see — export to Phoenix too.
22	
23	run_id propagation (ADR-0015 #79): wrap a run in run_trace_context(run_id) so
24	every span produced during the run carries the run_id as session.id + metadata,
25	cross-referencing MLflow / PostgresSaver / PROV-DM.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import atexit
31	import logging
32	import os
33	import signal
34	import sys
35	import threading
36	from contextlib import contextmanager
37	
38	# ADR-0002: traces carry SCF-derived content and must never leave the machine.
39	# The loopback guard is the single canonical validator in config.py, shared with
40	# Qdrant and MLflow (pass-5) so the data-sovereignty boundary is enforced
41	# identically everywhere; 0.0.0.0 is rejected (it binds all interfaces).
42	from ..config import LOOPBACK_HOSTS as _LOOPBACK_HOSTS  # noqa: F401  (kept for callers/tests)
43	from ..config import load_platform_config
44	from ..config import require_loopback as _require_loopback
45	
46	_log = logging.getLogger(__name__)
47	
48	# Cached registered provider — registration is idempotent within a process
49	# (repeat bootstrap calls return the same provider instead of double-registering
50	# the instrumentor, which would duplicate every span).
51	_PROVIDER = None
52	
53	
54	def _phoenix_cfg() -> dict:
55	    return load_platform_config()["observability"]["phoenix"]
56	
57	
58	def phoenix_base_url() -> str:
59	    """Return the Phoenix server base URL, e.g. http://localhost:6006."""
60	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
61	    return f"http://{cfg.get('host', 'localhost')}:{cfg.get('port', 6006)}"
62	
63	
64	def phoenix_endpoint() -> str:
65	    """Return the OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint the tracer provider exports to.
66	
67	    Validates loopback at the source (pass-5 #obs-wiring): this is public API and
68	    is called from the __main__ entrypoint and tests, not only from the already-
69	    guarded instrument_langchain(), so it must enforce its own ADR-0002 precondition."""
70	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
71	    _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "Phoenix OTLP export")
72	    return f"{phoenix_base_url()}/v1/traces"
73	
74	
75	def launch_server_command() -> str:
76	    """Return the shell command that starts the persistent Phoenix server.
77	
78	    Validates the bind host is loopback (#283): the server bind, not just the
79	    client export host, is what determines whether traces are reachable off-box.
80	    """
81	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
82	    host = _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "server bind")
83	    return f"phoenix serve --host {host} --port {cfg.get('port', 6006)}"
84	
85	
86	def instrument_langchain(project_name: str | None = None):
87	    """
88	    Register a global OTel tracer provider pointed at the Phoenix server (using a
89	    background BatchSpanProcessor) and enable LangChain tracing for this process.
90	
91	    Idempotent: the first call registers; subsequent calls return the cached
92	    provider without re-instrumenting (so spans are never duplicated).
93	
94	    Returns the tracer provider.
95	    """
96	    global _PROVIDER
97	    if _PROVIDER is not None:
98	        return _PROVIDER
99	
100	    from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
101	    from opentelemetry import trace as _trace
102	    from phoenix.otel import register
103	
104	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
105	    # ADR-0002 data sovereignty: refuse to export SCF-derived traces off-box.
106	    _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "OTLP export")
107	
108	    # Bound the OTLP/HTTP exporter so a downed Phoenix server cannot block process
109	    # teardown for the exporter's default 10s on the shutdown flush. The installed
110	    # OTel BatchProcessor.force_flush() ignores its timeout_millis argument, so the
111	    # only effective cap is at the exporter level (#277). Don't clobber an explicit
112	    # operator override.
113	    os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
114	
115	    provider = register(
116	        project_name=project_name or cfg.get("project_name", "hc-grc"),
117	        endpoint=phoenix_endpoint(),
118	        batch=True,                        # BatchSpanProcessor: buffer + async export
119	        set_global_tracer_provider=True,   # so reasoning_client spans export too
120	    )
121	    # set_tracer_provider can only succeed once per process; if a competing global
122	    # provider already won the race, register() still RETURNS the Phoenix provider
123	    # (so LangChain spans, passed it explicitly, still reach Phoenix) but the GLOBAL
124	    # provider is something else — and reasoning_client's module-level tracer resolves
125	    # through the global, so Tier-3 frontier spans (their ONLY record) would silently
126	    # vanish. Surface that collision instead of dropping them silently (#278).
127	    if _trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
128	        _log.warning(
129	            "Phoenix did not become the global OTel tracer provider — a competing "
130	            "provider was registered first. Tier-3 reasoning_client spans will not "
131	            "reach Phoenix. Ensure bootstrap_observability() runs before any other "
132	            "OTel instrumentation."
133	        )
134	    LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(tracer_provider=provider, skip_dep_check=True)
135	    # Flush buffered spans at process exit so the BatchSpanProcessor does not drop
136	    # the tail of a run on a clean exit / SIGINT (#215). atexit does NOT run on
137	    # SIGTERM (launchd/kill/container-stop), so also install a SIGTERM handler that
138	    # flushes then exits — otherwise the tail of a killed run is silently lost
139	    # (#281). Both flushes are bounded by the exporter timeout set above (#277).
140	    if hasattr(provider, "force_flush"):
141	        atexit.register(provider.force_flush)
142	        _install_sigterm_flush(provider)
143	    _PROVIDER = provider
144	    return provider
145	
146	
147	def _install_sigterm_flush(provider) -> None:
148	    """Flush + shut down the span processor on SIGTERM before the process exits.
149	
150	    Signal handlers can only be installed from the main thread; if we're not on it
151	    (e.g. instrumentation wired from a worker), skip silently — atexit still covers
152	    the clean-exit and SIGINT paths."""
153	    if threading.current_thread() is not threading.main_thread():
154	        return
155	
156	    def _handler(_signum, _frame):
157	        try:
158	            provider.force_flush()
159	            if hasattr(provider, "shutdown"):
160	                provider.shutdown()
161	        finally:
162	            sys.exit(0)
163	
164	    try:
165	        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
166	    except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - non-main-thread / unsupported
167	        pass
168	
169	
170	def bootstrap_observability():
171	    """
172	    Idempotently wire tracing for the current process. Called once by each run
173	    entrypoint (graph runners). Degrades to a no-op — never raising — when tracing
174	    is disabled by config, switched off via HCGRC_DISABLE_TRACING, or the
175	    instrumentation stack cannot be initialized. Returns the provider or None.
176	    """
177	    if os.environ.get("HCGRC_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip():
178	        return None
179	    try:
180	        cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
181	    except Exception:
182	        return None
183	    if not cfg.get("enabled", True):
184	        return None
185	    # Also point MLflow at the local store so experiment tracking is actually
186	    # configured in a run path (cross-store run_id correlation, #282). Best-effort.
187	    try:
188	        from ..tracking.mlflow_setup import configure_mlflow
189	        configure_mlflow()
190	    except Exception:
191	        pass
192	    try:
193	        return instrument_langchain()
194	    except Exception:
195	        # Missing deps, server unreachable at registration, etc. — tracing is
196	        # best-effort observability and must never block the research run.
197	        return None
198	
199	
200	@contextmanager
201	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None):
202	    """
203	    Context manager that stamps run_id onto every span produced within it, for
204	    cross-store correlation. Sets it two ways so BOTH span sources pick it up:
205	    OpenInference using_attributes (LangChain auto-spans → session.id) AND OTel
206	    baggage `hcgrc.run_id`, which the reasoning_client seam reads as a fallback so
207	    its manual T2/T3 spans carry the run_id even when the caller didn't pass it
208	    explicitly (#239). No-op if run_id is None or the helpers are unavailable.
209	    """
210	    if not run_id:
211	        yield
212	        return
213	    from contextlib import ExitStack
214	
215	    from opentelemetry import baggage, context
216	
217	    with ExitStack() as stack:
218	        try:
219	            from openinference.instrumentation import using_attributes
220	            stack.enter_context(using_attributes(session_id=run_id, metadata={"run_id": run_id}))
221	        except Exception:
222	            pass
223	        token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("hcgrc.run_id", run_id))
224	        try:
225	            yield
226	        finally:
227	            context.detach(token)

4	ADR-0002 data sovereignty: Qdrant runs as a local Docker container (see

7	population are the Embedding Agent's responsibility (agents/02-data/embedding-agent).
8	
9	Connection parameters come from configs/platform.yaml -> vector_store.
10	"""

12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	from typing import Any
15	
16	from ..config import load_platform_config, require_loopback
17	
18	
19	def _cfg() -> dict[str, Any]:
20	    return load_platform_config()["vector_store"]
21

23	def qdrant_url() -> str:
24	    """Return the Qdrant REST base URL, e.g. http://localhost:6333."""

35	    """
36	    Construct a QdrantClient from platform config.

38	    Requires the `qdrant-client` package and a running Qdrant container.
39	    """

42	    # Qdrant, mirroring the Phoenix guard (pass-5). config.py validates at load; this
43	    # is the point-of-use guard so any code path that builds a client is covered.
44	    # Validate BEFORE importing the optional client so the guard fires regardless of

1	"""
2	MLflow tracking setup for HC-GRC (roadmap Phase 0, deliverable #1).
3	
4	DATA SOVEREIGNTY (ADR-0002): MLflow runs against a LOCAL SQLITE backend — a
5	single file on the compute node (configs/platform.yaml ->
6	observability.mlflow.tracking_uri, default "sqlite:///mlflow.db"). No remote
7	tracking server, no SaaS — nothing leaves the machine.
8	
9	(MLflow 3 put the legacy filesystem store, e.g. "mlruns/", into maintenance
10	mode; it refuses writes without MLFLOW_ALLOW_FILE_STORE=true and receives no
11	new features. SQLite is the supported local backend and is equally
12	data-sovereign — one file, no daemon.)
13	
14	The SQLite backend needs NO running daemon: experiment data is written
15	directly to the .db file. A server is only needed to *view* runs, via
16	`mlflow ui --backend-store-uri <uri>` (see launch_ui_command()).
17	
18	run_id propagation (ADR-0015 #79): callers should tag every run with the
19	platform run_id so it cross-references PostgresSaver/Phoenix/PROV-DM:
20	    mlflow.set_tag("run_id", state["run_id"])
21	"""
22	
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import os
26	from pathlib import Path
27	
28	import mlflow
29	
30	from ..config import load_platform_config, repo_root, require_local_uri
31	
32	# Any non-sqlite backing-store URI passes through untouched. Includes DB schemes
33	# (postgres/postgresql/mysql/mssql) — a bare `postgres://` was previously not
34	# recognized and got mangled into a bogus local path (#253).
35	_REMOTE_SCHEMES = (
36	    "http://", "https://", "file:",
37	    "postgresql:", "postgres:", "mysql:", "mysql+", "mssql:", "mssql+",
38	)
39	_SQLITE_PREFIX = "sqlite:///"
40	
41	
42	def get_tracking_uri() -> str:
43	    """
44	    Resolve the MLflow tracking URI from platform config.
45	
46	    A relative SQLite path ("sqlite:///mlflow.db") or a bare relative directory
47	    ("mlruns") is resolved to an absolute location under the repo root, so the
48	    store is independent of the working directory. Absolute paths and remote
49	    schemes (http:, postgresql:, file:) pass through untouched.
50	    """
51	    cfg = load_platform_config()["observability"]["mlflow"]
52	    uri = cfg.get("tracking_uri", f"{_SQLITE_PREFIX}mlflow.db")
53	    if uri.startswith(_SQLITE_PREFIX):
54	        path = uri[len(_SQLITE_PREFIX):]
55	        if not os.path.isabs(path):
56	            path = str(repo_root() / path)
57	        return f"{_SQLITE_PREFIX}{path}"
58	    if uri.startswith(_REMOTE_SCHEMES) or os.path.isabs(uri):
59	        return uri
60	    return str(repo_root() / uri)
61	
62	
63	def configure_mlflow() -> str:
64	    """
65	    Point MLflow at the local store and select the platform experiment.
66	
67	    Idempotent — safe to call at the start of every run. Returns the resolved
68	    tracking URI for logging/verification.
69	    """
70	    cfg = load_platform_config()["observability"]["mlflow"]
71	    uri = get_tracking_uri()
72	    # ADR-0002 defense-in-depth: refuse to point MLflow at a remote tracking server
73	    # even if config validation was bypassed — experiment metadata is SCF-derived
74	    # and must not leave the box (pass-5). config.py validates the raw value at load;
75	    # this guards the resolved value at the actual set_tracking_uri() call site.
76	    require_local_uri(uri, "observability.mlflow")
77	    # A bare local directory (legacy file store) must exist; sqlite/remote do not.
78	    if not uri.startswith(_SQLITE_PREFIX) and not uri.startswith(_REMOTE_SCHEMES):
79	        Path(uri).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
80	    mlflow.set_tracking_uri(uri)
81	    mlflow.set_experiment(cfg.get("experiment_name", "hc-grc"))
82	    return uri
83	
84	
85	def launch_ui_command() -> str:
86	    """Return the shell command to view runs in the MLflow UI."""
87	    return f"mlflow ui --backend-store-uri {get_tracking_uri()}"
88	
89	
90	if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover - operational entrypoint
91	    resolved = configure_mlflow()
92	    print(f"MLflow configured. tracking_uri={resolved}")
93	    print(f"View runs with: {launch_ui_command()}")

740	def is_available(tier: Tier) -> bool:
741	    """
742	    Cheap, side-effect-light liveness check for a tier's backend, used by nodes
743	    that must degrade gracefully rather than fail the run.
744	
745	    Does NOT spend an LLM turn (the old probe burned a real Tier-3 frontier turn
746	    on every call, #174, and could hang with no timeout, #173/#30). T1 is always
747	    available (local computation). Honors HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING.
748	    """
749	    if tier is Tier.T1:
750	        return True
751	    if os.environ.get("HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING", "").strip():
752	        return False
753	    if tier is Tier.T2:
754	        # Hit Ollama's /api/tags — no generation, short timeout — and confirm the
755	        # configured model is actually pulled, not merely that the server is up (#210).
756	        import json as _json
757	        import urllib.request
758	        try:
759	            with urllib.request.urlopen(f"{T2_BASE_URL}/api/tags", timeout=3) as r:
760	                if r.status != 200:
761	                    return False
762	                names = [m.get("name", "") for m in _json.loads(r.read()).get("models", [])]
763	        except Exception:
764	            return False
765	        # Exact match only: a prefix match (e.g. configured llama3.1:8b vs a
766	        # pulled llama3.1:70b) would pass here but complete() would then fail as
767	        # "model not found" (#232). Ollama tags an untagged pull as ":latest".
768	        return any(n == T2_MODEL or n == f"{T2_MODEL}:latest" for n in names)
769	    if tier is Tier.T3:
770	        # The `claude` CLI must be present and an OAuth token / stored creds
771	        # available — no frontier turn spent.

774	            return False

776	            return True
777	        creds = os.path.expanduser("~/.claude/.credentials.json")
778	        return os.path.exists(creds)

1	"""
2	PostgresSaver configuration for HC-GRC.
3	
4	LangGraph uses the checkpointer to persist graph state between turns,
5	enabling interrupt/resume across the operator approval gates.
6	
7	Per ADR-0015 (#79): run_id propagates to PostgresSaver as thread_id.
8	Cross-store query procedure in RUNBOOK.md §5.
9	
10	LOCAL-FIRST CONSTRAINT: PostgreSQL instance runs on-device.
11	No state leaves the machine. SaaS checkpointers are disqualified.
12	
13	Connection config:
14	  Database: hcgrc
15	  Host: localhost (default)
16	  Port: 5432 (default)
17	
18	For Phase 0 testing: use MemorySaver (no Postgres required).
19	For Phase 1+: use PostgresSaver with the connection string below.
20	"""
21	
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import atexit
25	import os
26	import threading
27	
28	from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
29	
30	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes
31	# checkpoint schema migrations across concurrent initializers (get_checkpointer).
32	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4843_4752  # "HCGR"
33	
34	# Guards the process-local one-time-init bookkeeping below. The PG advisory lock
35	# serializes the DDL across PROCESSES, but the in-memory check-and-set guards
36	# (_atexit_registered, _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS) are not atomic on their own — two
37	# threads calling get_checkpointer() concurrently could both pass the check and
38	# double-register the atexit handler / double-run setup(). This lock makes those
39	# check-and-set sequences atomic within the process (pass-5).
40	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
41	
42	# Pools opened by get_checkpointer, closed once at process exit. A single atexit
43	# handler closes them all — registering one per call would accumulate handlers
44	# across repeated get_checkpointer() calls (#249).
45	_OPEN_POOLS: list = []
46	_atexit_registered = False
47	
48	# conn_strs whose schema bootstrap (advisory lock + setup() DDL + migration SELECT)
49	# has already run in this process. setup() is idempotent but not free — re-running
50	# it on every get_checkpointer() call takes a global advisory lock and replays the
51	# migration version query at each run start. Bootstrap once per process per schema;
52	# the advisory lock still guards the first-time cross-process migration race (#276).
53	_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS: set[str] = set()
54	
55	
56	def _safe_close(pool) -> None:
57	    """Close one pool, swallowing errors so a single failing close() does not abort
58	    cleanup of the remaining pools at interpreter exit (pass-6). close() ultimately
59	    calls a C finish() that can raise."""
60	    try:
61	        pool.close()
62	    except Exception:
63	        pass
64	
65	
66	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool) -> None:
67	    global _atexit_registered
68	    with _init_lock:
69	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
70	        if not _atexit_registered:
71	            atexit.register(lambda: [_safe_close(p) for p in _OPEN_POOLS])
72	            _atexit_registered = True
73	
74	# ── Connection string ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
75	
76	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
77	    """
78	    Return the PostgreSQL connection string.
79	
80	    Precedence: HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL env var → the checkpointing.{host,port,database}
81	    section of platform.yaml → localhost default. This makes the platform.yaml
82	    checkpointing config live instead of dead (#225). Never logged, never committed.
83	
84	    ADR-0002: the resolved URI MUST point at a loopback host — graph state holds the
85	    full corpus analysis / hypotheses / gate decisions and must not leave the box.
86	    The env-var path bypasses config-load validation, so it is guarded here too
87	    (point-of-use defense-in-depth, mirroring Qdrant/MLflow) (pass-6).
88	    """
89	    from .infrastructure.config import require_local_uri
90	
91	    env_url = os.environ.get("HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL")
92	    if env_url:
93	        return require_local_uri(env_url, "checkpointing (HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL)")
94	    try:
95	        from .infrastructure.config import load_platform_config
96	        cp = load_platform_config().get("checkpointing", {}) or {}
97	        host = cp.get("host", "localhost")
98	        port = cp.get("port", 5432)
99	        database = cp.get("database", "hcgrc")
100	        url = f"postgresql://{host}:{port}/{database}"
101	    except Exception:
102	        return "postgresql://localhost:5432/hcgrc"
103	    # Validate OUTSIDE the try so a non-loopback host raises rather than being
104	    # masked by the localhost fallback.
105	    return require_local_uri(url, "checkpointing")
106	
107	
108	def configured_checkpointer():
109	    """
110	    Return the checkpointer the platform config asks for: MemorySaver when
111	    checkpointing.backend == 'memory', else the pooled PostgresSaver (#226).
112	    Runners use this as the default instead of hardcoding in-memory.
113	    """
114	    backend = "postgres"
115	    try:
116	        from .infrastructure.config import load_platform_config
117	        backend = (load_platform_config().get("checkpointing", {}) or {}).get("backend", "postgres")
118	    except Exception:
119	        backend = "postgres"
120	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=(backend == "memory"))
121	
122	
123	# ── Checkpointer factory ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
124	
125	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False):
126	    """
127	    Return a configured checkpointer.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        use_memory: If True, return MemorySaver (Phase 0 testing, no Postgres needed).
131	                    If False, return PostgresSaver (Phase 1+ production).
132	
133	    Returns:
134	        MemorySaver | PostgresSaver
135	    """
136	    if use_memory:
137	        return MemorySaver()
138	
139	    try:
140	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
141	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
142	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
143	    except ImportError as e:
144	        raise ImportError(
145	            "langgraph-checkpoint-postgres and psycopg[pool] are required for PostgresSaver. "
146	            "Run: pip install 'langgraph-checkpoint-postgres' 'psycopg[binary,pool]'"
147	        ) from e
148	
149	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
150	    try:
151	        # A single raw Connection has no recovery: when the Postgres backend goes
152	        # away (node sleep/restart, idle timeout, network blip) the cached
153	        # connection is permanently broken and every subsequent checkpoint op
154	        # raises — silent loss of durability exactly during interrupt/resume across
155	        # the approval gates. Use a self-healing ConnectionPool instead: it runs a
156	        # liveness check on checkout and discards/replaces dead connections.
157	        # PostgresSaver natively accepts a ConnectionPool. (Hardening pass 1, #4.)
158	        pool = ConnectionPool(
159	            conn_str,
160	            min_size=1,
161	            max_size=8,
162	            kwargs={
163	                "autocommit": True,
164	                "prepare_threshold": 0,
165	                "row_factory": dict_row,
166	            },
167	            check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,
168	            open=True,
169	        )
170	        # PostgresSaver does not own the pool, so close it cleanly at process exit
171	        # (otherwise the pool's worker thread cannot be joined during interpreter
172	        # finalization, raising a noisy PythonFinalizationError). A single shared
173	        # atexit handler closes all pools — no per-call accumulation (#249).
174	        _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
175	        # Serialize schema migrations across processes with a session advisory
176	        # lock (pass-1 #33). The lock and the migration DDL MUST run on the SAME
177	        # connection — pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped, and a pool-backed
178	        # PostgresSaver.setup() would check out a *different* connection for the
179	        # DDL, leaving the lock ineffective (pass-3 #2). So run setup() through a
180	        # saver bound to the single locked connection, then return the pool-backed
181	        # saver for normal operation.
182	        # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. setup() is idempotent
183	        # but a redundant advisory lock + migration SELECT on every run start is
184	        # wasteful; the lock still protects the first-time cross-process race (#276).
185	        # _init_lock makes the in-process check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads
186	        # don't both run setup() (pass-5); the held lock also serializes the DDL,
187	        # which is fine — it runs at most once per conn_str per process.
188	        with _init_lock:
189	            if conn_str not in _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS:
190	                with pool.connection() as conn:
191	                    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
192	                    try:
193	                        PostgresSaver(conn).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
194	                    finally:
195	                        # Best-effort unlock: if this raised it would MASK a setup()
196	                        # exception (the one worth surfacing). The advisory lock is
197	                        # session-scoped and auto-releases when the connection returns
198	                        # to the pool, so swallowing here is safe (pass-7).
199	                        try:
200	                            conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
201	                        except Exception:
202	                            pass
203	                _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.add(conn_str)
204	        return PostgresSaver(pool)
205	    except Exception as e:
206	        raise RuntimeError(
207	            f"PostgresSaver connection failed. Is Postgres running on {conn_str}? "
208	            f"Error: {e}\n"
209	            "For Phase 0 testing without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
210	        ) from e

Perfect. I can see that:
1. `_t2_complete()` at line 254 validates with `require_local_uri()`
2. `get_postgres_connection_string()` at lines 93, 105 validates with `require_local_uri()`
3. `get_qdrant_client()` at line 46 validates with `require_loopback()`
4. Various Phoenix setup functions validate with `_require_loopback()`
5. `configure_mlflow()` at line 76 validates with `require_local_uri()`

But `is_available(Tier.T2)` at line 759 does NOT validate before making the HTTP request.

This is definitely the issue I need to report. Let me create my final findings:

findings.title: is_available(Tier.T2) makes unvalidated HTTP request, violating ADR-0002 loopback enforcement
findings.problem: The is_available(Tier.T2) function makes an HTTP request to T2_BASE_URL without validating that the URL is a loopback address first. If HCGRC_OLLAMA_BASE_URL is misconfigured to point to a remote host (e.g., via environment variable), the function will make an unvalidated network request to an external system. This violates ADR-0002 data-sovereignty requirements which mandate that SCF-derived content never leaves the host. In contrast, _t2_complete() at line 254 correctly validates T2_BASE_URL with require_local_uri() before any network operations.
findings.best_practice_violated: ADR-0002: Data Sovereignty — SCF-derived content must never leave the compute node. Defense-in-depth requires every network operation to validate loopback/local constraints at the point of use.
findings.proposed_fix:
Add loopback validation to is_available(Tier.T2) before the urllib.request.urlopen() call. Insert this line before line 759:
    from .infrastructure.config import require_local_uri
    require_local_uri(T2_BASE_URL, "Tier-2 Ollama (HCGRC_OLLAMA_BASE_URL) availability check")
This mirrors the validation in _t2_complete() and ensures the function refuses to probe non-loopback hosts, maintaining the ADR-0002 boundary.
